# Transcrição: 06-Professional AND Personal- Why Both Elements are Key

- **Fonte:** 06-Professional AND Personal- Why Both Elements are Key
- **Duração:** 02:49
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:49]

I know you're anxious to learn how to tell stories. This has been going on for a while. I've got one more thing to tell you before we can move on. As we move on in this course, I'm going to be asking you to do two things. We're going to be working on learning to tell business stories because that's why you're here. I want you to improve your business prospects, make piles and piles of money, find billions of customers, find the best people for your company. So I'm going to teach you how to tell business stories. But at the same time, I also want you to be telling personal stories. And I'm going to be breaking up the instruction into those two realms. It's important. The truth is this. I can teach you to tell an effective business story because for the last 10 years, I have been telling personal stories on stages and learning how to do that well. Truthfully, everything I use in personal storytelling is immediately applicable to business storytelling. I want you to be able to do both because both are one and the same, except there's one difference. When you tell personal stories, you get to practice all the time. I want you to be telling personal stories because telling a better story requires practice. You need to tell them again and again. You need to craft them again and again. It's hard to tell 500 business stories. People don't want to hear them and you probably don't have them. But you have 500 personal stories. You'll see what I mean soon. You have many, many, many personal stories to tell. And every one of those personal stories that you tell will give you a chance to practice, to get better as a storyteller. To understand that the skills and strategies required to tell an effective personal story will automatically transfer to the business story. So as we move through this course, I want you to do both. Your instinct is going to be to skip the personal stories because you're about making money and I get it. I understand it completely. I would do the same, but don't. Learn to tell personal stories. If you're an outstanding teller of personal stories, you will be an outstanding teller of the business stories you want to tell. So do both. As we move through this course, don't skip out on the personal stories. It's those stories where you're going to do the most work. It's those stories where you're going to find the most practice. It's those stories where you're going to find the most skill development. And honestly, it's those stories that you will find the most rewarding to tell. So let's move forward telling business stories and personal stories side by side so that at the end of this course, you are just a better storyteller.
